metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

Self-correction is ordinary Mistral Vibe behaviour: the agent invokes a tool (OpenAI-shape `tool_calls[]` with a nested `function.name`), the tool returns a failing result, and the model emits another `assistant` message with fresh `tool_calls[]` to retry. Vibe v2.19.0 runs a normal tool-use REPL, so a failing-then-corrected loop inside one turn is nothing special.

The scenario's three state assertions map cleanly onto the vibe parser (parser.go):

**Bullet 1 ÔÇö `ready Ôćĺ working`.** The first `user` line maps to `user_message` (parser.go:50-55, opens the turn); the first `assistant` line carrying `tool_calls[]` maps to `assistant_message` (parser.go:67-71) which keeps the session working.

**Bullet 2 ÔÇö state stays `working` across all retry cycles, no flicker.** This is the core invariant and it holds structurally. The parser only settles to `turn_done` (Ôćĺ ready) on a text-only assistant message with NO `tool_calls` (parser.go:72-75). Every retry cycle is MORE `assistant_message` (working) + `tool_result` events within the same working episode. A `tool` line maps to `tool_result` (parser.go:77-82) and ÔÇö unlike kiro/antigravity ÔÇö the vibe adapter reads NO status/error field, so it never sets `IsError` and a failing tool result is not a distinct signal; it is just continued activity that does not touch state. There is no permission/AskUser gate in this flow, so no `waiting` either. Hence the session stays continuously `working` from the first tool call until the terminal text-only assistant message.

**Bullet 3 ÔÇö final `working Ôćĺ ready`.** When the model stops calling tools and emits its final answer text (no `tool_calls`), the parser emits `turn_done` (parser.go:72-75) and the session settles to ready. If vibe instead asked the user it would surface as a plain user-facing message, but vibe has no distinct question channel the adapter reads, so the honest terminal state here is ready.

## Daemon sensor capture

Source is `FilesUnderRoot` over `~/.vibe/logs/session/<id>/messages.jsonl` (agent.go:31-37); the meta.json sidecar supplies cwd, model NAME, and context_tokens (sidecar.go:55-78). Every retry iteration is one append to messages.jsonl, so the whole arc is observable from the tailed transcript. `daemon=full` for the state topology this scenario asserts.

## Driver pillar ÔÇö gap:wait_turn

The interactive driver is freshly scaffolded: `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` (driver-interactive.sh) ÔÇö `wait_turn` is present only as a stubbed arm, not genuinely elicited, so recipe-lint reports a semantic gap for the recipe's `wait_turn` step. `record` must port SEAM 2 (turn-completion poll) from a reference driver before it can drive this cell. This is tooling work, not an observability limit ÔÇö the recipe + spec stand.
caveats: The vibe adapter sets NO IsError on tool_result (parser.go:77-82) ÔÇö there is no status field in the sample tool shape, unlike kiro/antigravity. So the daemon sees the retry as continued working activity, not a distinct error signal. The scenario's acceptance is purely state-topological (no-flicker + final ready), which does NOT require an error flag, so daemon stays full; but any future assertion wanting to distinguish 'errored then recovered' from 'two normal tool calls' cannot be satisfied for vibe.

PROVISIONAL on emission: whether vibe actually writes a text-only terminal assistant message (turn_done) after the recovery cycle ÔÇö vs. keeping tool_calls open ÔÇö is a property of what the agent emits, not something the parser read can confirm. A live record promotes this from provisional to settled.
Recording must use a deterministic failing-then-passing tool sequence (e.g. bash 'false' then bash 'echo recovered') so the retry count is reproducible across captures.
driver=gap:wait_turn: record must port the wait_turn seam (SEAM 2) into driver-interactive.sh before driving; DRIVE_ELICITS currently lists only send/slash/sleep.

sources.note: Role map: userÔćĺuser_message (50-55); assistant+tool_callsÔćĺassistant_message/working (67-71); text-only assistantÔćĺturn_done (72-75); toolÔćĺtool_result with NO IsError (77-82). The terminal-turn_done gate is what keeps the session working across retry cycles.
FilesUnderRoot over ~/.vibe/logs/session (31-37) ÔÇö every retry append is tailed; CommandPattern process match (27-30) binds the python vibe process.
meta.json sidecar Ôćĺ cwd, model NAME (config.active_model), context_tokens (55-78); no cost, timestamps, or control.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" ÔÇö wait_turn is a stubbed arm only, so the recipe's wait_turn is a semantic gap Ôćĺ driver=gap:wait_turn until record ports SEAM 2.
Feature: Self-correction iteration ÔÇö the three no-flicker state bullets the daemon pillar is judged against.
Live vibe CLI v2.19.0 (source under site-packages/vibe/); real transcripts under ~/.vibe/logs/session/ confirm the messages.jsonl + meta.json shape.

preconditions: vibe CLI on PATH (~/.local/bin/vibe, v2.19.0) and authenticated (Mistral API key)
irrlichd is recording (run-cell.sh isolated mode, or --attach against a --record daemon)
driver seam ported by record: SEAM 2 wait_turn poll (DRIVE_ELICITS currently lacks wait_turn ÔÇö gap:wait_turn)
the retry tool commands are auto-approved so neither attempt trips a permission gate
setup: Create a fresh empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ so the session's working_directory is unique
Vibe mints its own session_<ts>_<hash> id on launch; the daemon session_id is that directory name (single-birth)

script.text: Run bash 'false' first; if it fails, retry with bash 'echo recovered'. Then reply 'done'.

verify: events.jsonl shows exactly one readyÔćĺworking transition for the session_<ts>_<hash> session
events.jsonl contains at least 2 tool-call events inside the working span (the failing attempt and the recovery)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to ready between the failing tool_result and the recovery tool call
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting at any point during the working span
events.jsonl shows exactly one workingÔćĺready transition at the terminal text-only assistant message
meta.json sidecar supplies model mistral-medium-3.5 and a non-zero context_tokens on turn_done
